originalTitle: 'I did not expect it': Kenya's Sabastian Sawe welcomed home with jubilant celebrations
title: Kenya's Super Runner is Home! Hooray for Sabastian Sawe!
content:
Hello, super kids! Guess what? Something amazing just happened in Kenya! We have a real-life superhero, and his name is Sabastian Sawe! He did something super special, and everyone is cheering for him!

A Big Champion Comes Home!

Imagine a big party! Lots of happy people, big smiles, loud music, and bright balloons everywhere! That's what it looked like when our friend Sabastian came back home to Kenya. Everyone was so excited to see him! They clapped their hands and shouted his name. Some people even danced with joy! It was a very, very happy day for our country, Kenya. You could feel the happiness in the air! His family was there too, waiting for him. His mum, dad, brothers, and sisters were so proud. They gave him big, warm hugs! It was like a giant family celebration, but for the whole country! Everyone felt like they were part of his family's joy.

Who is Sabastian Sawe?

Sabastian Sawe is a kind and very strong man from Kenya. He loves to run! He trains very, very hard every single day. He runs in the sunshine, he runs when it's cool, and he always tries his very best. Running is like his special superpower! Did you know? Many super-fast runners come from Kenya! Our country is famous all over the world for having amazing athletes who run like the wind! Sabastian is one of our shining stars. He works really, really hard to be the best runner he can be. He wakes up early, eats healthy food, and practices for many hours. This helps him to be strong and fast for the big races.

Running Super Fast!

Sabastian just ran a super-duper long race. It's called a marathon. Can you imagine running for a very, very long time? A marathon is like running more than 42 big, big blocks! It's a huge challenge, even for grown-ups! And guess what? Sabastian ran this super-long race faster than anyone else has *ever* run it before in a proper competition! He finished in less than two hours! That's faster than watching two of your favorite cartoon movies back-to-back! He ran so fast, like a cheetah chasing its dinner! He kept going and going, even when his legs must have been tired, until he crossed the finish line. When he finished, everyone knew he had done something truly amazing. He made history for Kenya and for the world! Did you know? Running is great exercise! It makes your heart strong, helps you feel happy, and gives you lots of energy to play and learn! Maybe you can try running around your school field sometime!

A Party for Our Hero!

When Sabastian arrived back in Kenya, it was a massive party! People lined up along the roads just to cheer for him. They held up colourful signs that said 'Welcome Home, Hero!' and 'Well Done, Sabastian!' Everyone wanted to show their love. There were drums playing happy, bouncy music, and people were dancing in the streets. It felt like a big carnival! Everyone wanted to show Sabastian how proud they were of him and his incredible achievement. He even waved to everyone from a special car, smiling his biggest, happiest smile. He felt so much love from his country. His family and friends had tears of joy in their eyes. They knew how much hard work he put in. All those early mornings and long, tiring runs paid off! It was a moment to remember forever.

Dreams Come True!

Sabastian Sawe showed all of us that if you work hard, believe in yourself, and never give up, your dreams can come true. He dreamed of being a great runner, and he did it! He made Kenya super proud and showed the world what Kenyans can do. He is an inspiration for all of us. Maybe you like to run too! Or maybe you like to draw, or sing, or build amazing things with blocks. Whatever your dream is, remember Sabastian. Keep trying your best, work hard, and you can achieve wonderful things too! This happy welcome for Sabastian tells him that everyone in Kenya supports him and celebrates his success. It shows that we cheer for our heroes and their amazing talents, big or small. So, let's all say 'Hooray for Sabastian Sawe!' He is a true champion, a real Kenyan hero, and we are so, so happy he is home!
summary: Sabastian Sawe, a Kenyan runner, made history by running a super-fast marathon in less than two hours and received a huge, happy hero's welcome home from his family and country.
whyMatters: This is important because Sabastian's amazing run shows everyone in Kenya and the world that working hard can make dreams come true, making our country very proud and inspiring all kids to try their very best at whatever they love to do.

originalTitle: Iran miss Fifa congress but 'will play' at World Cup - Infantino
title: Iran miss Fifa congress but 'will play' at World Cup - Infantino
